question.text: Under Rule 9(9A) of the PML Rules, non-profit organisations seeking to open accounts must register on which portal?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Rule 9(9A) requires that every non-profit organisation which intends to have an account-based relationship with a reporting entity shall be registered on the DARPAN Portal of NITI Aayog. The records of such registration are required to be maintained for a period of 5 years after the account-based relationship is ended.
**The Trap:** SEBI portal is for capital market intermediaries. RBI CIMS is for banking regulatory filings. MCA21 is the Ministry of Corporate Affairs portal for companies and LLPs. None of these are the designated portal for NPO registration under PML Rules — it is specifically DARPAN of NITI Aayog.
**Cross-Exam Context:** NPOs are identified as potentially vulnerable to terrorist financing abuse under FATF standards. DARPAN registration provides a centralized database for monitoring and transparency of NGOs/NPOs, complementing AML/CFT due diligence requirements.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** NPOs = DARPAN Portal of NITI Aayog. Registration records must be maintained for 5 years after the end of the account relationship.
